title: La informalidad laboral subió al 43% en el cuarto trimestre de 2025, con mayor incidencia en mujeres y jóvenes
content: La tasa más alta de informalidad se observa entre las mujeres de hasta 29 años, donde el indicador alcanza el 57,9%; y las de 65 años y más, que tienen un índice de 61,6%.

title: El petróleo opera en torno a los u$s100 y Wall Street sube pese a la nueva escalada en Medio Oriente
content: Trump afirmó que Irán se puso en contacto con la Casa Blanca para entablar negociaciones de paz, mientras Estados Unidos iniciaba un bloqueo naval del estrecho de Ormuz en la séptima semana de la guerra.

title: RIMI: el Gobierno activa un salvavidas para las pymes, ¿cuándo estará disponible?
content: La reglamentación de la devolución anticipada de IVA fija un cupo del 50% sobre los fondos que el Presupuesto destine a la restitución de créditos fiscales del tributo en otros regímenes.

title: Cuánto sale un tender eléctrico: precios, alternativas y diferencias con la secadora
content: Que la humedad no te detenga a la hora de lavar la ropa. Conocé estas útiles alternativas para el secado rápido.

title: La billetera virtual que ofrece 10% de descuento en farmacias y sin tope de reintegro
content: Entre sus ofertas disponibles, la herramienta digital presenta oportunidades especiales en farmacias y perfumerías seleccionadas.

title: Cómo pagar en ARBA antes del 14 de abril la cuota 2 del Impuesto Inmobiliario Urbano con descuento de hasta el 10%
content: Se puede abonar desde el sitio web oficial del organismo con tarjetas de crédito, código para cajeros automáticos o Cuenta DNI.

title: Los depósitos en dólares pierden impulso pese a la Ley de Inocencia Fiscal y emerge una señal sobre bimonetarismo
content: Aunque los depósitos en dólares crecieron tras la nueva normativa, el impulso pierde fuerza y surgen dudas sobre su efectividad. En paralelo, el sistema financiero refleja un equilibrio entre pesos y divisas que responde más a cobertura que a dolarización.

title: Carta abierta de pymes al Gobierno: califican al modelo de "antiproductivo" y piden medidas urgentes
content: En una misiva, empresarios del sector de la industria, el comercio, la construcción, las economías regionales y los servicios detallan cómo está en estos momentos la cadena productiva.

title: RIMI para pymes: punto por punto, las claves del nuevo régimen de inversiones
content: El Gobierno reglamentó el RIMI con beneficios fiscales y de acceso para pymes que inviertan en producción, tecnología y eficiencia energética. El esquema estará vigente por dos años y fija requisitos clave para acceder a la devolución de IVA y otros incentivos.

title: Cuál es la diferencia entre RIMI y RIGI: a quiénes está enfocado cada uno y el impacto en la economía argentina
content: El nuevo esquema amplía el alcance de los beneficios fiscales para proyectos productivos en Argentina.

Wave of $15 Billion US IPOs Runs Headlong Into War’s New Phase
content:
Investment bankers are hitting the road to raise more than $15 billion across initial public offerings in the coming weeks, nervously eyeing market volatility as a standoff with Iran threatens a fragile ceasefire.
The success of a string of deals, led this week by a $2.23 billion IPO for ventilation and filtration systems provider Madison Air Solutions Corp., will be closely watched. Bankers are looking to put choppy performance for recent debutants in the rear-view mirror, clearing a path for the mammoth SpaceX listing expected in June. Michael Ventura, Co-Head of US Equity Capital Markets at RBC joins to discuss with Bailey Lipschultz and Carol Massar on Bloomberg Businessweek Daily. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: New Zealand Dairy Farmers Pocket Billions From Huge Capital Payout
content: New Zealand dairy farmers are about to get a record multi-billion dollar, tax-free payout. They may need every cent of it.

title: Most Emerging Assets Gain After Hormuz Blockade Spurs Oil Spike
content: Most emerging-market currencies climbed on Monday after oil retreated from session highs when President Donald Trump said Iran had reached out seeking a deal, even as the US began enforcing a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: JPMorgan's Mulvihill: Sentiment Hitting Private Credit
content: JPMorgan Asset Management Global Alternatives Strategist Aaron Mulvihill discusses private credit continuing to pay a premium, and why investors are starting to lean into asset classes that pay a constant return.  He talks with katie Greifeld and Isabelle Lee on "the Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Eases as US Plan to Blockade Hormuz Raises Inflation Risks
content: Gold edged down amid mounting inflation concerns after US President Donald Trump’s order to blockade the Strait of Hormuz deepened the global energy-supply shock.

title: KBW CEO Michaud Says Bank Buybacks May Surprise Investors
content: KBW CEO Thomas Michaud discusses how economic resilience is leaving banks well-positioned and the potential impact from the war in Iran. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: S&P Erases Losses Since Iran War Began | Closing Bell
content: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Katie Greifeld, Isabelle Lee, Carol Massar and Bailey Lipschultz. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Won’t Leave Without Iran’s Uranium, Bass Says
content: Kyle Bass, Rochefort co-CEO, says Iran has not been negotiating with the US in good faith and he says the US won’t leave without Iran’s highly enriched uranium that can be used to build a nuclear bomb. He speaks on “Bloomberg The Close.”

title: Nemetschek Strikes Deal for Thoma’s Software Firm HCSS
content: Nemetschek SE agreed to acquire construction management software provider Heavy Construction Systems Specialists from Thoma Bravo.

title: Goldman Extends Borrowing Run With $6.5 Billion Bond Sale
content: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. raised $6.5 billion from a US investment-grade bond sale, extending a borrowing spree that included a record debt offering earlier this year.

content: Final prewar cargoes to leave Gulf set to unload in coming days, escalating a global rush to secure barrels

title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
content: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

content: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
content: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
content: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
content: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.
